narrative:
On July 14, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 44.1%, proceeding with 1,109 out of 2,512 scheduled cases across 40 active judges. This indicates a marginal improvement in efficiency compared to the previous day (July 13), when the court registered a 42.8% hearing rate, disposing of or hearing 1,205 out of 2,816 scheduled cases. 

Individual judge workloads varied significantly. Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the heaviest single list, hearing 112 out of 229 scheduled cases (48.9%). The bench of the Chief Justice & K.S. Hemalekha achieved a strong 72.9% hearing rate in Hall 1, proceeding with 43 of 59 cases. Conversely, some scheduled lists saw low activity; Justice Vijaykumar A. Patil in Hall 32 did not proceed with any of his 52 scheduled cases, and Justice B M Shyam Prasad had a major list of 91 cases in Hall 6 go unheard, though he cleared smaller lists in the same hall.

Stage-wise conversion metrics reveal that "Orders" remained the most dominant and successful category, converting 416 out of 815 scheduled cases (51.0%), a notable increase from the 43.0% conversion rate observed on July 13. "Final Hearing" stages, which historically lag, converted only 24.7% (127 of 514 cases), though this is a slight improvement over the previous day's 21.2%. 

In terms of case types, Writ Petitions (WP) continued to dominate the docket with 993 scheduled cases, achieving a 41.0% hearing rate (up from 37.9% on July 13). Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) and Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) showed steady conversion at 50.8% and 46.5% respectively, while Regular First Appeals (RFA) lagged with a conversion rate of just 22.3%.
